---
title: "ML Methods: Salary Prediction"
format:
  html:
    theme: cerulean
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    embed-resources: true
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
date-modified: today
date-format: long
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: true
  freeze: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Use Multiple Linear Regression to model salary based on location, job title, skills, remote type, employment type, minimum experience, and education level.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, when, trim, regexp_replace, count as spark_count
from pyspark.ml.feature import StringIndexer, OneHotEncoder, VectorAssembler
from pyspark.ml.regression import LinearRegression
from pyspark.ml import Pipeline
from pyspark.ml.evaluation import RegressionEvaluator
from IPython.display import display
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

spark = SparkSession.builder \
    .config("spark.driver.host", "localhost") \
    .config("spark.driver.memory", "4g") \
    .config("spark.executor.memory", "4g") \
    .appName("SalaryMLR") \
    .getOrCreate()

spark.catalog.clearCache()

# Load prepared model-ready dataset from data_cleaning.qmd
df_model_base = spark.read.parquet("data/prepared/salary_regression_data.parquet")
skill_count_features_df = spark.read.parquet("data/prepared/skill_count_features.parquet")

df_model_base = df_model_base.join(
    skill_count_features_df,
    df_model_base.id == skill_count_features_df.job_id,
    how="left"
).drop("job_id")

# Add education level from cleaned dataset
education_df = (
    spark.read.parquet("data/cleaned/lightcast_cleaned.parquet")
    .select(
        col("ID").alias("id"),
        col("EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME").alias("education_level")
    )
    .dropDuplicates(["id"])
)

df_model_base = (
    df_model_base
    .join(education_df, on="id", how="left")
    .withColumn(
        "education_level",
        trim(
            regexp_replace(
                regexp_replace(col("education_level"), r"[\n\r]", " "),
                r"\s+",
                " "
            )
        )
    )
    .fillna({"education_level": "Unknown"})
)

# Keep only valid salary rows
df_salary = df_model_base.filter(
    col("salary").isNotNull() &
    (col("salary") > 0) &
    (col("salary") <= 500000)
).cache()

# Group job titles using top 30 titles, same idea as friend's model
top_titles = [
    r["title_clean"] for r in
    df_salary.groupBy("title_clean")
    .agg(spark_count("*").alias("cnt"))
    .orderBy(col("cnt").desc())
    .limit(30)
    .collect()
]

df_model = df_salary.withColumn(
    "title_grouped",
    when(col("title_clean").isin(top_titles), col("title_clean")).otherwise("Other")
)

# Actual skill flags prepared in data_cleaning.qmd
skill_cols = [
    "has_python",
    "has_sql",
    "has_machine_learning",
    "has_tableau",
    "has_power_bi",
    "has_aws",
    "has_excel",
    "has_sap",
    "common_skill_count",
    "specialized_skill_count",
    "software_skill_count"
]

# Select seven feature groups:
# 1 location, 2 job title, 3 skills, 4 remote type, 5 employment type, 6 min experience, 7 education
df_model = (
    df_model
    .select(
        ["salary",
         "state_name",
         "title_grouped",
         "remote_type_name",
         "employment_type_name",
         "min_years_experience",
         "education_level"] + skill_cols
    )
    .fillna({
    "state_name": "Unknown",
    "title_grouped": "Other",
    "remote_type_name": "Unknown",
    "employment_type_name": "Unknown",
    "min_years_experience": 0,
    "education_level": "Unknown",
    "has_python": 0,
    "has_sql": 0,
    "has_machine_learning": 0,
    "has_tableau": 0,
    "has_power_bi": 0,
    "has_aws": 0,
    "has_excel": 0,
    "has_sap": 0,
    "common_skill_count": 0,
    "specialized_skill_count": 0,
    "software_skill_count": 0
    })
.cache())

print("Model rows:", df_model.count())
print("Top 30 grouped titles:", top_titles[:10], "...")

# Categorical encoders
title_indexer = StringIndexer(
    inputCol="title_grouped",
    outputCol="title_idx",
    handleInvalid="keep"
)
title_encoder = OneHotEncoder(
    inputCol="title_idx",
    outputCol="title_vec",
    dropLast=False
)

state_indexer = StringIndexer(
    inputCol="state_name",
    outputCol="state_idx",
    handleInvalid="keep"
)
state_encoder = OneHotEncoder(
    inputCol="state_idx",
    outputCol="state_vec",
    dropLast=False
)

remote_indexer = StringIndexer(
    inputCol="remote_type_name",
    outputCol="remote_idx",
    handleInvalid="keep"
)
remote_encoder = OneHotEncoder(
    inputCol="remote_idx",
    outputCol="remote_vec",
    dropLast=False
)

employment_indexer = StringIndexer(
    inputCol="employment_type_name",
    outputCol="employment_idx",
    handleInvalid="keep"
)
employment_encoder = OneHotEncoder(
    inputCol="employment_idx",
    outputCol="employment_vec",
    dropLast=False
)

education_indexer = StringIndexer(
    inputCol="education_level",
    outputCol="education_idx",
    handleInvalid="keep"
)
education_encoder = OneHotEncoder(
    inputCol="education_idx",
    outputCol="education_vec",
    dropLast=False
)

assembler = VectorAssembler(
    inputCols=[
        "title_vec",
        "state_vec",
        "remote_vec",
        "employment_vec",
        "education_vec",
        "min_years_experience"
    ] + skill_cols,
    outputCol="features"
)

lr = LinearRegression(
    featuresCol="features",
    labelCol="salary"
)

pipeline = Pipeline(stages=[
    title_indexer, title_encoder,
    state_indexer, state_encoder,
    remote_indexer, remote_encoder,
    employment_indexer, employment_encoder,
    education_indexer, education_encoder,
    assembler, lr
])

train, test = df_model.randomSplit([0.8, 0.2], seed=42)

model = pipeline.fit(train)
predictions = model.transform(test)

# Metrics
r2 = RegressionEvaluator(
    labelCol="salary",
    predictionCol="prediction",
    metricName="r2"
).evaluate(predictions)

rmse = RegressionEvaluator(
    labelCol="salary",
    predictionCol="prediction",
    metricName="rmse"
).evaluate(predictions)

mae = RegressionEvaluator(
    labelCol="salary",
    predictionCol="prediction",
    metricName="mae"
).evaluate(predictions)

lr_model = model.stages[-1]
n = predictions.count()
p = len(lr_model.coefficients)

adjusted_r2 = 1 - ((1 - r2) * (n - 1) / (n - p - 1)) if n > p + 1 else None

metrics_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["R²", "Adjusted R²", "RMSE", "MAE"],
    "Value": [
        round(r2, 4),
        round(adjusted_r2, 4) if adjusted_r2 is not None else None,
        round(rmse, 2),
        round(mae, 2)
    ]
})

display(metrics_df)

# Coefficient table
coefficients = lr_model.coefficients.toArray()

title_labels = list(model.stages[0].labels) + ["title_unseen"]
state_labels = list(model.stages[2].labels) + ["state_unseen"]
remote_labels = list(model.stages[4].labels) + ["remote_unseen"]
employment_labels = list(model.stages[6].labels) + ["employment_unseen"]
education_labels = list(model.stages[8].labels) + ["education_unseen"]

feature_names = (
    ["Title: " + x for x in title_labels] +
    ["State: " + x for x in state_labels] +
    ["Remote: " + x for x in remote_labels] +
    ["Employment: " + x for x in employment_labels] +
    ["Education: " + x for x in education_labels] +
    ["Minimum Years Experience"] +
    ["Skill: " + x.replace("has_", "").replace("_", " ").title() for x in skill_cols]
)

feature_names = feature_names[:len(coefficients)]

coef_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature": feature_names,
    "Coefficient": coefficients
})

coef_df = coef_df[
    ~coef_df["Feature"].str.contains("unseen", case=False, na=False)
].sort_values("Coefficient", ascending=False)

print("\n=== Salary Impact by Feature ===")
display(coef_df)

print(f"\nIntercept: ${lr_model.intercept:,.0f}")
print(f"Test R²: {r2:.4f}")
print(f"Test Adjusted R²: {adjusted_r2:.4f}" if adjusted_r2 is not None else "Adjusted R²: not available")
```

## Which Features Impact Salary?
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

coef_plot_df = coef_df.copy()
coef_plot_df["abs_coef"] = coef_plot_df["Coefficient"].abs()

top_coef_plot = (
    coef_plot_df
    .sort_values("abs_coef", ascending=False)
    .head(20)
    .sort_values("Coefficient")
    .copy()
)

top_coef_plot["direction"] = top_coef_plot["Coefficient"].apply(
    lambda x: "Positive salary association" if x > 0 else "Negative salary association"
)

top_coef_plot["label"] = top_coef_plot["Coefficient"].apply(
    lambda x: f"${x:,.0f}"
)

def shorten_feature_name(x):
    if x.startswith("Education: "):
        x = x.replace("Education: ", "Edu: ")
        x = x.replace("High school or GED", "HS/GED")
        x = x.replace("Associate degree", "Assoc.")
        x = x.replace("Bachelor's degree", "Bachelor")
        x = x.replace("Master's degree", "Master")
        x = x.replace("Ph.D. or professional degree", "PhD")
        x = x.replace(", ", " + ")

    if x.startswith("Title: "):
        title = x.replace("Title: ", "")
        if len(title) > 42:
            title = title[:42] + "..."
        x = "Title: " + title

    if len(x) > 70:
        x = x[:70] + "..."

    return x

top_coef_plot["Feature_short"] = top_coef_plot["Feature"].apply(shorten_feature_name)

# Make y-axis labels unique so Plotly does not stack duplicate labels
top_coef_plot["Feature_display"] = top_coef_plot.apply(
    lambda r: f"{r['Feature_short']}  ({r['label']})",
    axis=1
)

fig_coef = px.bar(
    top_coef_plot,
    x="Coefficient",
    y="Feature_display",
    color="direction",
    orientation="h",
    title="Top Salary Predictors by Absolute Coefficient",
    labels={
        "Coefficient": "Estimated Salary Impact ($)",
        "Feature_display": ""
    },
    color_discrete_map={
        "Positive salary association": "#2ca02c",
        "Negative salary association": "#d62728"
    },
    hover_data={
        "Feature": True,
        "Coefficient": ":,.0f",
        "direction": False,
        "Feature_short": False,
        "Feature_display": False,
        "label": False,
        "abs_coef": False
    }
)

fig_coef.update_layout(
    template="plotly_white",
    height=900,
    width=1150,
    margin=dict(l=520, r=80, t=90, b=70),
    showlegend=True,
    legend=dict(
        orientation="h",
        yanchor="bottom",
        y=1.03,
        xanchor="right",
        x=1
    ),
    title=dict(x=0.02, xanchor="left"),
    xaxis=dict(
        title="Estimated Salary Impact ($)",
        tickprefix="$",
        separatethousands=True,
        gridcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0.08)"
    ),
    yaxis=dict(
        title="",
        automargin=True
    )
)

fig_coef.show()
```

### Try different job features and skills to see how each feature changes predicted salary.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| results: asis

import json
from html import escape
from IPython.display import HTML

# Collect simple option lists
state_options = sorted([x for x in df_model.select("state_name").distinct().toPandas()["state_name"].dropna().tolist()])
title_options = sorted([x for x in df_model.select("title_grouped").distinct().toPandas()["title_grouped"].dropna().tolist()])
remote_options = sorted([x for x in df_model.select("remote_type_name").distinct().toPandas()["remote_type_name"].dropna().tolist()])
employment_options = sorted([x for x in df_model.select("employment_type_name").distinct().toPandas()["employment_type_name"].dropna().tolist()])
education_options = sorted([x for x in df_model.select("education_level").distinct().toPandas()["education_level"].dropna().tolist()])

# Create coefficient lookup
coef_lookup = dict(zip(feature_names, coefficients))

payload = {
    "intercept": float(lr_model.intercept),
    "coef": {k: float(v) for k, v in coef_lookup.items()},
    "states": state_options,
    "titles": title_options,
    "remotes": remote_options,
    "employments": employment_options,
    "educations": education_options,
    "skills": [
    "has_python",
    "has_sql",
    "has_machine_learning",
    "has_tableau",
    "has_power_bi",
    "has_aws",
    "has_excel",
    "has_sap"
]
}

payload_json = json.dumps(payload)

html = f"""
<div id="salary-widget" style="border:1px solid #ddd; border-radius:12px; padding:18px; max-width:850px; font-family:Arial, sans-serif;">
  <h3 style="margin-top:0;">Salary Prediction Feature Explorer</h3>
  <p style="color:#555;">Select job characteristics and toggle skills to see how model features change predicted salary.</p>

  <div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:14px;">
    <label>State<br><select id="sw-state" style="width:100%; padding:6px;"></select></label>
    <label>Job Title Group<br><select id="sw-title" style="width:100%; padding:6px;"></select></label>
    <label>Remote Type<br><select id="sw-remote" style="width:100%; padding:6px;"></select></label>
    <label>Employment Type<br><select id="sw-employment" style="width:100%; padding:6px;"></select></label>
    <label>Education Level<br><select id="sw-education" style="width:100%; padding:6px;"></select></label>
    <label>Minimum Years Experience<br><input id="sw-years" type="number" value="0" min="0" step="1" style="width:100%; padding:6px;"></label>
  </div>

  <h4>Skills Mentioned in Posting</h4>
  <div id="sw-skills" style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:8px;"></div>

  <div style="margin-top:18px; padding:14px; background:#f8f9fa; border-radius:10px;">
    <div style="font-size:13px; color:#666;">Predicted Salary</div>
    <div id="sw-prediction" style="font-size:34px; font-weight:bold;">$0</div>
    <div id="sw-factors" style="margin-top:12px; font-size:14px;"></div>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
const salaryModel = {payload_json};

function addOptions(selectId, values) {{
  const el = document.getElementById(selectId);
  values.forEach(v => {{
    const opt = document.createElement("option");
    opt.value = v;
    opt.textContent = v;
    el.appendChild(opt);
  }});
}}

function prettySkill(s) {{
  const names = {{
    "has_python": "Python",
    "has_sql": "SQL",
    "has_machine_learning": "Machine Learning",
    "has_tableau": "Tableau",
    "has_power_bi": "Power BI",
    "has_aws": "AWS",
    "has_excel": "Excel",
    "has_sap": "SAP"
  }};
  return names[s] || s.replace("has_", "").replaceAll("_", " ");
}}

function money(x) {{
  return "$" + Math.round(x).toLocaleString();
}}

function updatePrediction() {{
  let pred = salaryModel.intercept;
  let factors = [];

  const state = document.getElementById("sw-state").value;
  const title = document.getElementById("sw-title").value;
  const remote = document.getElementById("sw-remote").value;
  const employment = document.getElementById("sw-employment").value;
  const education = document.getElementById("sw-education").value;
  const years = parseFloat(document.getElementById("sw-years").value || "0");

  const featureKeys = [
    "State: " + state,
    "Title: " + title,
    "Remote: " + remote,
    "Employment: " + employment,
    "Education: " + education
  ];

  featureKeys.forEach(k => {{
    const c = salaryModel.coef[k] || 0;
    pred += c;
    if (Math.abs(c) > 1) factors.push([k, c]);
  }});

  const yearCoef = salaryModel.coef["Minimum Years Experience"] || 0;
  pred += yearCoef * years;
  if (Math.abs(yearCoef * years) > 1) factors.push(["Minimum Years Experience", yearCoef * years]);

  salaryModel.skills.forEach(s => {{
    const box = document.getElementById("sw-" + s);
    if (box && box.checked) {{
      const key = "Skill: " + prettySkill(s);
      const c = salaryModel.coef[key] || 0;
      pred += c;
      if (Math.abs(c) > 1) factors.push([key, c]);
    }}
  }});

  document.getElementById("sw-prediction").textContent = money(pred);

  factors.sort((a,b) => Math.abs(b[1]) - Math.abs(a[1]));
  const topFactors = factors.slice(0,5).map(([k,c]) => {{
    const arrow = c >= 0 ? "↑" : "↓";
    const color = c >= 0 ? "#2ca02c" : "#d62728";
    return `<div style="color:${{color}};">${{arrow}} ${{k}} (${{money(c)}})</div>`;
  }}).join("");

  document.getElementById("sw-factors").innerHTML =
    "<strong>Top factors in this prediction</strong>" + topFactors;
}}

addOptions("sw-state", salaryModel.states);
addOptions("sw-title", salaryModel.titles);
addOptions("sw-remote", salaryModel.remotes);
addOptions("sw-employment", salaryModel.employments);
addOptions("sw-education", salaryModel.educations);

const skillsDiv = document.getElementById("sw-skills");
salaryModel.skills.forEach(s => {{
  const label = document.createElement("label");
  label.innerHTML = `<input type="checkbox" id="sw-${{s}}"> ${{prettySkill(s)}}`;
  skillsDiv.appendChild(label);
}});

document.querySelectorAll("#salary-widget select, #salary-widget input").forEach(el => {{
  el.addEventListener("change", updatePrediction);
  el.addEventListener("input", updatePrediction);
}});

updatePrediction();
</script>
"""

display(HTML(html))
```
